headline: Mag 7 loses $2.3T but chipmakers still backed
detail: AI spending jitters are hitting the biggest tech names hard, which drags sentiment across your entire book. The silver lining specifically called out is chipmakers holding investor support - directly relevant to SMH, SOXL, and your CRWD/QQQ/SPY positions. This is a bifurcation: broad tech under pressure, semis relatively defended.
rec: Watch SMH closely - if it slides toward $580, your SOXL position accelerates the pain 3x on margin. No new buys warranted yet; let the AI spending narrative settle before adding semi exposure.

headline: Strait of Hormuz reopening faster than expected, Morgan Stanley cuts oil target
detail: Faster Hormuz reopening reduces geopolitical risk premium in oil, which is broadly positive for risk assets and tech specifically since lower energy costs ease inflation pressure. Morgan Stanley cutting their oil target reinforces this. Indirectly supportive for your names via macro sentiment.
rec: This is a soft tailwind for your book but not a direct catalyst to buy or trim anything. No action needed - just a mild positive backdrop for risk-on tech positioning.

headline: Digital Realty falls 5% on $3.5B Blackstone data center stake
detail: A major data center name getting hit 5% on a large capital deployment signals the market is scrutinizing AI infrastructure spending costs hard right now. This feeds directly into the AI spending jitter narrative hurting Mag 7 and could weigh on sentiment for your semi and cloud-adjacent names. Not a direct holding but sets the mood for your sector.
rec: No direct trade, but this reinforces caution on adding new semi or AI-infrastructure positions today. Hold current levels and watch if this spread to broader chip sentiment pressures SMH below $600.

headline: European central banker warns inflation to stay significantly above target
detail: Persistent European inflation keeps global rate-cut hopes in check and strengthens the higher-for-longer narrative, which historically pressures high-multiple tech and growth names - exactly your book. On margin, rate sensitivity is amplified because your borrowing costs stay elevated too.
rec: No immediate trade triggered, but this is a reminder not to over-lever into new positions. If U.S. inflation data echoes this tone, your QQQ and growth names face multiple compression. Hold stops, do not add margin exposure today.
